zh.Higham Ferrers, Northamptonshire, Englan: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾希加姆 費雷斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Cordray II於大約1484年出生在英國維爾爾特時，他的父親Thomas Cordray25歲，母親Joan Grey17歲。他於大約1509年與Joan Seymour結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1546年2月16日之前。

zh-hans.Higham Ferrers, Northamptonshire, Englan: 英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾希加姆 費雷斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Cordray II于大约1484年出生在英国維爾爾特时，他的父亲Thomas Cordray25岁，母亲Joan Grey17岁。他于大约1509年与Joan Seymour结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1546年2月16日之前。

nameMeanings.name: Cordray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French coer de roi ‘(having the) heart of a king’. This surname is now extinct in Britain.

variants.name: Cordrey
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Cordray . Alternatively, possibly derived from Old French corderie ‘ropery, rope-walk’, taken to mean a worker at a ropery.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Cordray II was born about 1484, in Chute, Wiltshire, England, his father, Thomas Cordray, was 25 and his mother, Joan Grey, was 17. He married Joan Seymour about 1509. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died before 16 February 1546.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Cordray II est né environ 1484, à Chute, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Cordray, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan Grey, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Joan Seymour environ 1509. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé avant 16 février 1546.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Cordray II(이)가 대략 1484년 영국 윌츠히르 c후트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Cordray(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Grey(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1509년 Joan Seymour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1546년 2월 16일 이전 사망했다.
